Paste multi-line coords, an OzRunways route, or Google Earth KML → auto-fills rows.

Drag ⋮⋮ to reorder. ⌘/Ctrl+Enter to plot.

MBZ Zones

Mandatory Broadcast Zones — advisory. Verify each zone's frequency, radius and vertical limits against the current PNG AIP / OzRunways before operational use.

Copy Route to Clipboard

For ForeFlight
Tap Copy → open ForeFlight on iPad → tap FPL → Enter Route → paste. Universal Clipboard syncs Mac → iPad automatically.

ForeFlight Route String

DDMMSS format — works directly in ForeFlight's route field

OzRunways Route String

Hemisphere-first DMS — paste into OzRunways → Plan → Quick Plan → Go

For reference / saved waypoint identifiers

Shareable Link

Send to your phone (AirDrop / Messages) → open it to load this whole plan. Works offline.

Settings

Corridor each side of route
Range ring on hover / tap
Corridor sets how far either side of your route NM Plotter looks for airfields & frequencies. The range ring is the circle drawn when you hover or tap a field.
Flight recording
Manual records when you tap the record button on the map. Auto starts recording on its own once you're moving (GPS tracking must be on) and stops after you've been stopped a while. Tracks are saved to the Flight Log on this device and export as GPX.
Heads-upiOS pauses background tabs, so recording only runs while NM Plotter is open with the screen on. The screen is kept awake during recording where supported. This is a personal track log, not a certified flight recorder.
Cloud route library
Type the same phrase on each device (Mac & iPad); it's stored on this device only. Plan on the desktop, tap ☁ Save to Cloud; then at the aircraft open Saved Routes on the iPad and Pull it. Pulling never happens on its own. Test connection
Guided tour
A quick walkthrough of the main controls, highlighted one at a time. Desktop only for now.
Advisory onlyOpen-source data, not AIRAC-current. Cross-check every frequency against the PNG AIP before use. Pilot-entered freqs show a green ✓.
